summary: Kenneth Suna compares covered call premiums across stocks of varying volatility, demonstrating that stable dividend-paying stocks (Coca-Cola, JNJ, McDonald's) generate 1-1.86% weekly premiums but require large capital outlays, while riskier stocks like HIMS generate 4.4% weekly premiums. He emphasizes understanding individual risk tolerance and warns that early assignment on dividend stocks can cause investors to miss dividend payments.
takeaways: Stable stocks like Coca-Cola yield ~1.24% premium per week on $6,900-$7,000 capital
HIMS generates 4.4% weekly premium but carries elevated volatility risk
Johnson & Johnson pays only ~0.6% premium on $16,000 capital required
Waste Management yields 1.86% monthly (not weekly) on $22,586 capital
Monitor X-dividend dates closely when selling covered calls; early assignment forfeits dividend rights
Higher premiums correlate directly with stock volatility; stable stocks = lower premiums but more predictable risk

strategy_candidates.change: Apply volatility-tiering to Premium Desk: allocate 60% capital to stable dividend stocks (target 1-1.5% weekly, $600-750/mo) and 40% to higher-volatility names (target 3.5-4.5% weekly, $500-600/mo) to reach $1,250/mo target with managed risk
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates $600/week achievable with volatile stocks ($15-20k deployed) but shows stable stocks (KO, JNJ, MCD) are predictable 1-1.86% generators; combining both tiers mathematically reaches Brian's $1,250/mo goal while reducing concentration risk

title: Covered Calls and Dividends
